title: Stdlib M8.1 integration: math_linalg.ri + dimensional_consistency.ri
analysis: Task 313 creates two .ri fixture files and a Rust integration test that exercises stdlib math/linalg builtins (from tasks 300+301) through the full parse->compile->eval pipeline. Key constraints discovered during analysis: (1) .ri list literals [a,b,c] produce Value::List, not Value::Tensor/Vector -- must use vec3() stdlib constructor for linalg operations; (2) No Newton unit literal exists -- compound dimensions must be built via arithmetic (e.g., 1kg * 1m / (1s * 1s) for FORCE); (3) No prelude constants (pi, g, e) -- must use numeric literals (9.81 for gravity); (4) tensor_components_f64() requires Value::Tensor|Point|Vector (not List) for dot/cross/normalize/magnitude; (5) vec3() constructor requires all args to share the same physical dimension, so zero-valued dimensioned scalars (0.0 * n_unit) are needed for zero components. The test file follows the established chained_comparison_eval.rs pattern: helper fn loads .ri file, parses, compiles, evaluates; individual #[test] functions assert on specific ValueCellId entries. All stdlib functions (dot, cross, normalize, magnitude, clamp, lerp, acos, mod, vec3) already exist from dependencies 300-301; this task verifies they work correctly end-to-end through the .ri language pipeline.

steps.description: Create crates/reify-eval/tests/m8_stdlib_integration.rs with eval_ri_file(path) helper (read_to_string -> parse -> compile -> eval with MockConstraintChecker) and math_linalg_parses_and_compiles test that loads ../../examples/math_linalg.ri and asserts parse+compile have no errors and eval produces non-empty values. Test fails because .ri file does not exist.

Create examples/math_linalg.ri with structure MathLinalg containing: unit helpers (n_unit=1kg*1m/(1s*1s), zero_n=0.0*n_unit, zero_m=0.0*1m, v_unit=1m/1s, zero_v=0.0*v_unit), force=vec3(10.0*n_unit, zero_n, zero_n), displacement=vec3(5.0*1m, zero_m, zero_m), work=dot(force, displacement), r_arm=vec3(zero_m, 2.0*1m, zero_m), f_applied=vec3(3.0*n_unit, zero_n, zero_n), torque=cross(r_arm, f_applied), velocity=vec3(3.0*v_unit, 4.0*v_unit, zero_v), speed=magnitude(velocity), direction=normalize(velocity), unit_x=vec3(1.0, 0.0, 0.0), unit_y=vec3(0.0, 1.0, 0.0), angle_between=acos(dot(normalize(unit_x), normalize(unit_y))), chained=clamp(lerp(1mm, 10mm, 0.3), 2mm, 8mm).

Write math_linalg_dot_product_work test: extract ValueCellId(MathLinalg, work), assert Value::Scalar with si_value approx 50.0 and dimension == FORCE.mul(&DimensionVector::LENGTH) (energy: m^2*kg*s^-2). Write math_linalg_cross_product_torque test: extract torque, assert it is Vector/Tensor with 3 components, z-component is Scalar with si_value approx -6.0 and dimension == DimensionVector::LENGTH.mul(&FORCE), x and y components have si_value approx 0.0.

Run cargo test -p reify-eval --test m8_stdlib_integration. Verify dot product and cross product tests pass. If vec3 dimension consistency fails (e.g., zero components not matching), fix math_linalg.ri to ensure all vec3 args share dimensions.

Write math_linalg_normalize_and_magnitude test: assert speed is Scalar with si_value approx 5.0 and dimension == LENGTH.div(&DimensionVector::TIME) (velocity). Assert direction is Vector with 3 Real (dimensionless) components approximately [0.6, 0.8, 0.0]. Write math_linalg_angle_and_chained test: assert angle_between is Scalar with si_value approx PI/2 and dimension == DimensionVector::ANGLE. Assert chained is Scalar with si_value approx 0.0037 (3.7mm in SI metres) and dimension == DimensionVector::LENGTH.

Run cargo test -p reify-eval --test m8_stdlib_integration. Verify normalize, magnitude, angle, and clamp+lerp tests pass. Fix math_linalg.ri if any edge cases arise (e.g., acos domain issue, lerp t parameter not recognized as dimensionless).

Add dimensional_consistency_parses_and_compiles test that loads ../../examples/dimensional_consistency.ri and asserts parse+compile have no errors and eval produces non-empty values. Test fails because .ri file does not exist.

Create examples/dimensional_consistency.ri with structure DimensionalConsistency containing: n_unit=1kg*1m/(1s*1s), lerp_mm=lerp(5mm, 15mm, 0.5), m_vec=vec3(2.0*1m, 1.0*1m, 0.0*1m), n_vec=vec3(3.0*n_unit, 0.0*n_unit, 0.0*n_unit), dot_result=dot(m_vec, n_vec), cross_result=cross(m_vec, n_vec), g_accel=9.81*1m/(1s*1s), weight=g_accel*1kg, mod_result=mod(7, 3), norm_result=normalize(vec3(3.0*1m, 4.0*1m, 0.0*1m)), mag_result=magnitude(vec3(3.0*1m, 4.0*1m, 0.0*1m)), clamped_dim=clamp(3mm, 1mm, 10mm).

Write dimension assertion tests: (1) dim_lerp_preserves_length: lerp_mm has si_value approx 0.010 (10mm) and dimension==LENGTH; (2) dim_dot_energy: dot_result has si_value approx 6.0 and dimension==LENGTH.mul(&FORCE); (3) dim_cross_torque: cross_result z-component has si_value approx -3.0 and dimension==LENGTH.mul(&FORCE); (4) dim_gravity_force: weight has si_value approx 9.81 and dimension==FORCE; (5) dim_mod_integer: mod_result is Value::Int(1); (6) dim_normalize_dimensionless: norm_result components are Value::Real (not Scalar); (7) dim_magnitude_preserves: mag_result has si_value approx 5.0 and dimension==LENGTH; (8) dim_clamp_preserves: clamped_dim has si_value approx 0.003 and dimension==LENGTH.

Run full test suite: cargo test -p reify-eval --test m8_stdlib_integration. Verify all dimensional consistency tests pass. Fix dimensional_consistency.ri if any dimension mismatches or evaluation ordering issues arise.

design_decisions.decision: Use vec3() stdlib constructor for vectors instead of list literals [a,b,c]
design_decisions.rationale: .ri list literals compile to CompiledExprKind::ListLiteral which evaluates to Value::List. The stdlib linalg functions (dot, cross, normalize, magnitude) use tensor_components_f64() which only accepts Value::Tensor|Point|Vector. The vec3() constructor creates Value::Vector, making it compatible with all linalg operations.

design_decisions.decision: Build compound dimensions via Scalar arithmetic instead of N/J unit literals
design_decisions.rationale: The compiler's unit_to_scalar only supports mm, cm, m, in, deg, rad, kg, g, s. There is no Newton (N) or Joule (J) unit literal. Force dimension is constructed as 1kg * 1m / (1s * 1s), which correctly produces Scalar with FORCE dimension through eval_mul's Scalar*Scalar path (si_value multiplication, dimension exponent addition).

design_decisions.decision: Use numeric literal 9.81 for gravitational acceleration instead of a prelude constant
design_decisions.rationale: The stdlib.md documents constants (pi, g, e, etc.) but they are not yet implemented as evaluatable prelude values in the compiler or expression evaluator. The 'g' unit suffix in the compiler means grams (mass), not gravitational acceleration. let g_accel = 9.81 * 1m / (1s * 1s) produces Scalar(9.81, FORCE/MASS) = acceleration.

design_decisions.decision: Create .ri files as example fixtures loaded by a Rust test harness
design_decisions.rationale: Following the established pattern from m5_integration.rs and e2e_bracket.rs: .ri files live in examples/, Rust tests load them via fs::read_to_string('../../examples/...'), then parse->compile->eval->assert. The .ri language has no assertion mechanism, so value and dimension checking must be done in Rust.

design_decisions.decision: Use zero-valued dimension helpers (zero_n, zero_m, zero_v) for vec3 zero components
design_decisions.rationale: vec3() (construct_point_or_vector) requires all 3 components to share the same physical dimension. A bare 0.0 is Real (dimensionless) which would fail dimension consistency checks against Scalar components. Using 0.0 * n_unit produces Scalar(0.0, FORCE) matching other FORCE-dimensioned components.

design_decisions.decision: Single Rust test file (m8_stdlib_integration.rs) for both .ri fixtures
design_decisions.rationale: Both .ri files test the same stdlib feature set (math + linalg builtins from M8.1). Grouping them in one test module with clear section comments follows the pattern of m5_integration.rs. The eval_ri_file helper is shared across all tests.

reuse.how: Reuse the identical pattern: load source, reify_syntax::parse(source, ModulePath::single(name)), reify_compiler::compile(&parsed), Engine::new(MockConstraintChecker, None).eval(&compiled). Adapted to read from file instead of string literal.
Import reify_test_support::mocks::MockConstraintChecker for Engine construction. No geometry kernel needed (pass None) since tests are pure math/linalg evaluation.

reuse.what: ValueCellId for value extraction from EvalResult
reuse.how: Use ValueCellId::new('MathLinalg', 'work') etc. to index into result.values HashMap. Same pattern used in all existing integration tests (m5_integration.rs, boundary5_engine.rs, chained_comparison_eval.rs).

reuse.what: DimensionVector constants and arithmetic for assertions
reuse.where: crates/reify-types/src/dimension.rs: DimensionVector::LENGTH, FORCE, ANGLE, TIME, MASS
reuse.how: Use .mul() and .div() to compute expected compound dimensions: FORCE.mul(&LENGTH) for energy/torque, LENGTH.div(&TIME) for velocity. Use DimensionVector::DIMENSIONLESS for normalize checks.

reuse.what: vec3() stdlib constructor for .ri vector creation
reuse.how: Call vec3(x, y, z) in .ri source to create Value::Vector. All 3 args must be numeric with matching dimension. Compatible with dot/cross/normalize/magnitude via tensor_components_f64().

Follow the structure definition pattern: 'structure Name { let binding = expr ... }'. Use unit literals (mm, m, kg, s) for dimensioned scalar values. Use function call syntax fn_name(args) for stdlib builtins.
